festivals.name: Fes Festival of World Sacred Music
festivals.description: Ten nights of Sufi music, qawwali, and sacred world music fill Fes's ancient madrasas and plazas.

festivals.name: Tan-Tan Moussem
festivals.description: A UNESCO-recognized gathering of nomadic Sahrawi tribes with camel races, music, and traditional crafts.

destinations.name: Marrakech
destinations.description: The Red City's medieval medina bursts with souks, riads, and the world's most chaotic square, Djemaa el-Fna.

destinations.name: Fes
destinations.description: The world's largest car-free urban zone contains the medieval Fes el-Bali with famous leather tanneries.

destinations.name: Sahara Desert (Merzouga)
destinations.description: The towering Erg Chebbi dunes rise from the Sahara — ride camels to a Berber camp for unforgettable stargazing.

destinations.name: Chefchaouen
destinations.description: The famous blue-washed mountain town cascades down the Rif hillside in a maze of indigo alleyways.

destinations.name: Atlas Mountains (Toubkal)
destinations.description: North Africa's highest peak at 4,167m surrounded by Berber villages, walnut groves, and ancient mule tracks.

destinations.name: Essaouira
destinations.description: A windy blue-and-white UNESCO port city with a Portuguese fortress, thriving art scene, and great seafood.

destinations.name: Todra Gorge
destinations.description: A dramatic limestone canyon where 300m walls narrow to 10m, famous for rock climbing and palm-shaded springs.

destinations.name: Rabat
destinations.description: Morocco's elegant capital is a UNESCO World Heritage city with a 12th-century kasbah and ancient Roman ruins at Sala Colonia.
